Dear Mr. Lumsdon: 
Enforcement!Conflict of Interest 
322~6441 
I am writing this letter to confirm our conversation 
of June 3, 1976. 
With respect to Northridge Fashion Center, 
the facts, as I understand them, are as follows: 
Northridge Fashion Center is a California corporation 
which owns and operates a shopping center in Los Angeles. 
The Northridge Fashion Center recently became the property 
of the David Gladstone Foundation which is operating North-
ridge as a fully funded charitable trust. 
Mr. Jones, your 
client, is one of three trustees administering the Gladstone 
Foundation. 
The shares of Northridge Fashion Center stock 
are voted by a majority vote of the Gladstone Foundation 
trustees. 
Mr. Jones also is the president of Northridge 
Fashion Center and has served in that capacity continuously, 
beginning at a time prior to the devise of Northridge to the 
Gladstone Foundation. 
With respect to R. J. Investments, it is my understand-
ing that it is a California corporation owned by two share-
holders. 
Mr. Jones is the majority shareholder and the presi-
dent. 
You also indicated that Mr. Jones would appreciate 
interim advice which would permit him to make contributions 
while awaiting a final opinion from the Commission. 
Any con-
tributions made by Mr. Jones and R. J. Investments must be 
cumulated for the purpose of determining whether Mr. Jones 
and R. J. 
InvesL~ents must file campaign statements as a 
committee pursuant to Government Code Section 82013. 
However, 
contributions by the North 
Fashion Center need not be 
added to those of Mr. Jones and R. J. Investments, unless 
there is an agreement or mutual un 
rstanding, implied or ex-
pressed, that contribut 
North 
and Mr. Jones and/or 
R. J. Inves 
nts will be cantr 
with

to

Is. 


Thomas Lumsdon 
Page Two 
If the opinion reaches a result different from the 
advice contained herein, Mr. Jones will not be subject to 
an enforcement action by the Corr~ission so long as he has 
provided the Commission with all of the material facts and 
has relied upon the advice received. 
I should caution you, 
however, that after issuance of the opinion 
Mr. Jones might 
be required to file campaign statements as a committee dis-
closing financial transactions which occur between now and 
the adoption of a formal opinion. 
TP :pvp 
If you have any further questions, please contact me. 
Sincerely, 
Ted Prim 
Staff Counsel 
Legal Division

sections.conclusion: Contributions made by Mr. Jones and R.J. Investments must be cumulated for purposes of determining whether they must file campaign statements as a committee pursuant to Government Code Section 82013. However, contributions by Northridge Fashion Center need not be added to those of Mr. Jones and R.J. Investments unless there is an agreement or mutual understanding, implied or expressed, that contributions by Northridge Fashion Center and Mr. Jones and/or R.J. Investments will be contributed with a unified purpose to influence political candidates or ballot measures.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether contributions made by Mr. Jones and R.J. Investments must be cumulated together with contributions by Northridge Fashion Center for purposes of determining campaign statement filing obligations under Government Code Section 82013.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that Mr. Jones's and R.J. Investments' contributions must be cumulated together, but Northridge Fashion Center's contributions need not be cumulated unless there is an agreement or mutual understanding to contribute with a unified political purpose.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers but contains clear interim advice on cumulation requirements. Some text is illegible/truncated at document margins. Letter confirms prior telephone conversation and provides protection from enforcement action if opinion later differs, provided all material facts were disclosed.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether contributions made by Mr. Jones and R.J. Investments must be cumulated together with contributions by Northridge Fashion Center for purposes of determining campaign statement filing obligations under Government Code Section 82013.

CONCLUSION: Contributions made by Mr. Jones and R.J. Investments must be cumulated for purposes of determining whether they must file campaign statements as a committee pursuant to Government Code Section 82013. However, contributions by Northridge Fashion Center need not be added to those of Mr. Jones and R.J. Investments unless there is an agreement or mutual understanding, implied or expressed, that contributions by Northridge Fashion Center and Mr. Jones and/or R.J. Investments will be contributed with a unified purpose to influence political candidates or ballot measures.

embedding.summary: FPPC staff counsel provides interim advice to Mr. Thomas G. Lumsdon regarding contribution cumulation requirements for his client Mr. Jones, who holds interests in both R.J. Investments and serves as trustee of the Northridge Fashion Center. The letter clarifies which contributions must be cumulated for campaign statement filing purposes under Government Code Section 82013.
